document: RBI Master Direction on Information Technology Framework for NBFC Sector (2017) (India - Row 15)

sections.heading: As you are aware, the Income Declaration Scheme, 2016 (the Scheme)   has
sections.text:
2. As you are aware, the Income Declaration Scheme, 2016 (the Scheme)   has 
come into effect from June 1, 2016 and some declarants may like to pay their tax 
dues in cash. In this connection, it has been brought to our notice by the 
Government that banks are hesitant in allowing deposit of large amounts of cash by 
the declarants under the Scheme, with them, for credit to Government account.

sections.heading: We advise that banks must invariably accept cash, irrespective of amount, over
sections.text:
3. We advise that banks must invariably accept cash, irrespective of amount, over
the counters from all declarants who desire to deposit cash at the counters, including 
deposits under the above Scheme through challan ITNS- 286. In this connection, 
banks shall comply with the Know Your Customer requirements for customers and 
walk-in customers as contained in Master Direction – Know Your Customer 
Withdrawn

RBI,DBR, CO                           - 2 -                                               continuation sheet   
                    
Direction, 2016 issued vide DBR.AML.BC.No.81/14.01.001/2015-16 dated February 
25, 2016.
